one_line_thesis: Adobe is a subscription compounder with $23.77B FY2025 revenue and $9.85B free cash flow; at $222.65 synthesis IRR is 33.28% per year, above our bar, but generative-AI competition warrants monitoring.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Adobe is a subscription software compounder with exceptional gross margins in Digital Media and a large enterprise marketing stack in Digital Experience. FY2025 free cash flow was $9.85 billion ($23.07 per share on 427 million diluted shares), operating income $8.71 billion, and the company returned $11.28 billion via share repurchases. At $222.65 per share (NASDAQ close 2026-07-09), we expect 32.0% per year over seven years on filed FY2025 owner cash (Lawrence base). Proof-first component schedule sums to $656 per share base case versus price ~$223. Durable competitive advantage (Munger mo...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Entry prices were computed mechanically from the routed power-zone profile (High-return compounder). They are decision inputs, not a decision; the owner must review the scenarios before acting.
pricing_analysis.strongest_counter_explanation: growth projected without its capital cost
pricing_analysis.economic_claim_note: Per-share claims use fully diluted shares from valuation.inputs.

trigger.policy: A complete economic-value analysis is required when any trigger fires; otherwise it is recommended.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of ADBE, including Digital Media and Document Cloud owner cash, Digital Experience engine, Firefly AI monetization option, net financial claims, and AI competition reserve.
economic_claim.unit_source: FY2025 weighted average diluted shares 427.0M per ADBE/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260115_rpt20251128_acc0000796343_26_000003.htm; inputs.shares_millions in valuation.json.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Segment engines value revenue-weighted owner cash once; Firefly option is a separate milestone band; net financial claims and AI reserve use unique overlap keys. Consolidated free cash flow is not double-counted across segments.

economic_value_bridge.accounting_reference: FY2025 10-K and Q2 FY2026 10-Q filing extracts; GAAP net income is cross-check only for subscription software compounder.

component_groups.label: Digital Media and Document Cloud owner-cash engine
component_groups.economic_claim: Digital Media and Document Cloud owner-cash engine
component_groups.valuation_basis: Revenue-weighted owner cash capitalization on Creative Cloud and Acrobat.
component_groups.adjustments: Publishing segment cash embedded in residual allocation.

component_groups.label: Digital Experience Cloud owner-cash engine
component_groups.valuation_basis: Revenue-weighted owner cash capitalization on enterprise marketing stack.
component_groups.adjustments: Competitive share shifts versus Salesforce and Google reflected in multiple band.

component_groups.label: Firefly and generative-AI monetization option
component_groups.valuation_basis: Risk-adjusted milestone value beyond segment capitalization multiples.
component_groups.adjustments: Not in Lawrence base free cash flow path; bull sensitivity only.

risk_and_timing.timing_basis: Generative-AI credit monetization and ARPU lift over 3 to 7 years; Firefly embedded in Creative Cloud since September 2023.
risk_and_timing.probability_basis: Judgment from FY2025 RPO $22.5B and subscription mix; Adobe does not disclose standalone Firefly revenue.
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Incremental generative-AI model training and inference spend embedded in consolidated R&D and cost of revenue.

component_groups.label: Net cash and debt claims on common equity
component_groups.economic_claim: Net cash and debt claims on common equity
component_groups.valuation_basis: Cash $5.43B less long-term debt $6.21B with operating liquidity reserve.
component_groups.adjustments: January 2025 note offering adds leverage for buybacks.

component_groups.label: Generative-AI competition and stock-based compensation reserve
component_groups.economic_claim: Generative-AI competition and stock-based compensation reserve
component_groups.valuation_basis: Negative reserve for seat churn, SBC dilution, and multiple compression.

limitations: Revenue-weighted free cash flow split is approximate; Adobe does not disclose segment cash flow.
Firefly milestone band and AI competition reserve remain widest judgment components.

decision.next_action: Complete isolated rater outputs, proposer case, pre-mortem, and research response without sharing peer votes.

probability_and_timing.timing_basis: Generative-AI credit monetization and ARPU lift over 3 to 7 years; Firefly embedded in Creative Cloud since September 2023.
probability_and_timing.probability_basis: Judgment from FY2025 RPO $22.5B and subscription mix; Adobe does not disclose standalone Firefly revenue.
probability_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Incremental generative-AI model training and inference spend embedded in consolidated R&D and cost of revenue.

options.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Complete isolated rater outputs, proposer case, pre-mortem, and research response without sharing peer votes.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: Component changes are classified from observable differences in quantity, comparable method, probability, timing, capital, evidence, and schedule scope.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

timeline.label: Basic EPS moved +34.6% versus the prior period (12.4 to 16.7).
Operating income moved +29.1% versus the prior period (6,741.0 to 8,706.0).
Q2 FY2026: H1 revenue $13.02B (+12.3% YoY); diluted EPS $8.86 H1 — growth re-accelerating
FY2025 10-K: revenue $23.77B, FCF $9.85B, RPO $22.52B, $11.28B share repurchases
$2.0B senior notes offering (2028/2030/2035 tranches) to fund returns and general corporate purposes
FY2024 10-K filed: revenue $21.51B; operating income recovery continues post-2023 restructuring
Terminated Figma acquisition after regulatory opposition; paid $1.0B break fee — design collaboration remains competitive gap
Firefly generative AI commercially embedded across Creative Cloud — AI product cycle begins
Announced Figma acquisition ($20B) — later terminated; signaled push into collaborative design
Completed shift to subscription-only Creative Cloud — recurring revenue model fully in place
Shantanu Narayen CEO (since 2007) — long-tenured leadership through cloud transition
Acquired Omniture — foundation of Digital Experience Cloud enterprise marketing stack
Acquired Macromedia (Flash, Dreamweaver) — expanded creative suite breadth
PDF format standardized via Acrobat — document franchise established

industry.structure: Creative and marketing software is oligopolistic at the professional tier (Adobe dominant in design/PDF) with fragmented challengers in prosumer and generative-AI niches. Enterprise marketing clouds compete with Salesforce, Google, and Oracle.
industry.share_shift: Adobe is holding professional creative share while low-cost tools (Canva, AI image generators) attack individual and SMB tiers; Experience Cloud competes for enterprise marketing budgets with mixed win rates.
industry.trend: Generative AI is embedding into creative workflows; subscription RPO and AI credit monetization are reshaping pricing; regulatory scrutiny blocked the Figma deal, leaving collaborative design as an open competitive front.
